title: The Linux Story Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Linux fundamentals, distributions, and the Linux philosophy.

questions.question: What percentage of the world's top servers run Linux?
questions.explanation: Linux powers over 96.3% of the world's top 1 million servers, making it the dominant server operating system.

questions.question: What is the relationship between the Linux kernel and a Linux distribution?
options: The kernel is the core; a distro bundles it with tools and a package manager
A distro is a lighter version of the kernel
The kernel is only for advanced users
questions.explanation: The Linux kernel manages hardware and processes. A distribution packages the kernel with user-space tools, libraries, and a package manager into a complete OS.

questions.question: Which Linux distribution is recommended for DevOps beginners?
questions.explanation: Ubuntu has the largest community, the most tutorials, and is the most common choice for cloud servers, making it ideal for beginners.

questions.question: Which package manager does Alpine Linux use?
questions.explanation: Alpine Linux uses apk (Alpine Package Keeper) as its package manager. It's known for its tiny footprint, making it popular for Docker containers.

questions.question: Docker containers share the host Linux kernel.
questions.explanation: Docker containers share the host OS kernel, which is why they're lightweight compared to virtual machines. This is fundamentally a Linux technology.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT part of the Linux philosophy?
questions.explanation: The Linux philosophy favors the command line and text-based interfaces. While GUIs exist, the core philosophy centers on composable CLI tools and text streams.

questions.question: Who created the Linux kernel?
questions.explanation: Linus Torvalds created the Linux kernel in 1991. Richard Stallman created GNU, Dennis Ritchie co-created Unix and C, and Ken Thompson co-created Unix.

questions.question: What command shows your current working directory?
questions.explanation: The 'pwd' (print working directory) command displays the full path of your current directory.

questions.question: In Linux, network sockets can be represented as files.
questions.explanation: One of the core Linux principles is 'everything is a file.' Devices, processes, and even network sockets are represented as files in the filesystem.

questions.question: What is WSL?
questions.explanation: WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) allows you to run a full Linux terminal environment inside Windows without needing a virtual machine.
